title: Resident Meeting on Sacramento Nightlife
description: Residents are invited to share thoughts, concerns, and suggestions on Sacramento's nighttime economy for the Sociable City Assessment, hosted by the City's Office of Nighttime Economy and the Responsible Hospitality Institute.

title: Sacramento 2040 Draft Plan Orientation Webinar
description: A 90-minute orientation webinar on the Draft 2040 General Plan and Climate Action & Adaptation Plan, with an overview, participation info, and Q&A. Spanish and Chinese translation available.

title: Neighborhood Clean Up
description: Walk the neighborhood picking up trash with your neighbors; trash pickers, bags, and gloves provided. Sign-in with coffee and pastries from 8:30 AM.

title: National Night Out Progressive Potluck
location: 22nd St median, 2431 H St courtyard & Marshall Park
description: Progressive potluck dinner for National Night Out with appetizers, entree, and dessert courses at three neighborhood locations. Meet neighbors, police officers, city officials, and elected representatives.

title: Beyond Homeless Movie Screening
description: Outdoor screening of the documentary Beyond Homeless, hosted by Hope for Sacramento, about the homelessness crisis and San Antonio's Haven for Hope campus shelter model. Bring family and snacks.

title: Pop Up Art Gallery
description: One-day pop-up art gallery with local artists, vendors, food, and drink, hosted by Reynen and Bardis Homes.

title: Ribbon Cutting for New Bikeways
description: Clean Air Day ribbon cutting celebrating the new 19th and 21st Street bikeway extensions, with remarks from local officials and an optional bike ride around the block.

title: Missing Middle Housing Webinars
description: First of two City orientation webinars on the Missing Middle Housing study's preliminary recommendations, covering built form. A second webinar on attainability and ownership follows October 19.

title: Neighborhood Cleanup and Tree Planting
description: Neighborhood trash pickup walk plus tree planting alongside park staff; trash pickers, bags, and gloves provided. Coffee and pastries from 8:30 AM.

title: District 4 Community Meeting
location: Mirasol Village Community Room, 1200 Richards Blvd
description: Councilmember Valenzuela's District 4 community meeting to connect with city staff and neighbors, with updates on projects and policies. Light refreshments; RSVPs encouraged.

title: MNENA Board Meeting
description: Board meeting with discussions on the future of Old Marshall School, homeless issues, Sutter's Landing Park, and more.

title: April Board Meeting
description: Busy board meeting with discussions on the future of Old Marshall School, homeless issues, Sutter's Landing Park, and more.

title: Urban Bicycling and Scooter 101 Class
description: Free City of Sacramento class covering bicycling and scooter laws, riding on streets and through intersections, avoiding crashes, and enjoying the ride.

title: Friends of Sutter's Landing Work Day
description: Volunteer work day with Friends of Sutter's Landing: raking up cut weeds and clearing around native plants in the 3.2-acre restoration site. Bring a rake and small tarp if you can.

title: Neighborhood Cleanup
location: Society Church at Leland Stanford Park, 27th & B Street
description: Walk the neighborhood picking up trash with your neighbors; trash pickers, bags, and gloves provided. Coffee and pastries from 8:30 AM.

title: Sutter's Fort Demonstration Day
description: Demonstration Day with hands-on stations, volunteer demonstrations, and staff-led presentations, hosted by California State Parks and Friends of Sutter's Fort. Includes complimentary admission to the State Indian Museum.

title: National Night Out
location: 22nd St median, 2431 H St courtyard & Marshall Park
description: Progressive potluck dinner for National Night Out with appetizer, entree, and dessert courses at three neighborhood locations. Chat with neighbors and meet police officers, city officials, and elected representatives.

title: National Night Out
location: 22nd St median, 2431 H St courtyard & Marshall Park
description: Reminder for the National Night Out progressive potluck dinner with three courses at three neighborhood locations, hosted by the Marshall-New Era and Boulevard Park associations.

title: McKinley Park Trash Dash
location: McKinley Park, by the duck pond
description: Family-friendly monthly Trash Dash, this quarter sponsored by the City's Recycling and Solid Waste division, focusing on the Northern Alhambra Corridor. Trash grabbers and bags provided; coffee, donuts, and pizza on MESA.

title: Water Discovery Day
description: City Department of Utilities event with guided tours of the water treatment plant and booths explaining how the City collects, treats, and distributes drinking water. Registration required for tours.

title: Midtown Halloween Festival & Pooch Parade
description: Afternoon festival with pups in costumes, live music, games, food, prizes, and the beloved pooch parade. Register your dog early.

title: Neighborhood Halloween Movie Night & Trunk-or-Treat (Save the Date)
description: Save-the-date for the BPNA and MNENA Halloween event: a spooky trunk-or-treat with games, activities, and snacks, followed by a family-friendly outdoor movie night.

title: Neighborhood Halloween Movie Night & Trunk-or-Treat
description: BPNA and MNENA host a spooky trunk-or-treat with games, activities, and snacks, followed by a haunted family-friendly outdoor movie night.

title: Accessible Afternoon Exploring the American River with Mobility Devices
location: Sutter's Landing Regional Park, front of Skate Park (20 28th St)
description: Free Lionheart Community event to explore the American River trails and beach using accessible mobility devices, including Freedom Trax, Action Track Chairs, the OMEO, SwinCars, and Paraboards.

title: Return of the Salmon Talk at Sutter's Landing
description: Laura Drath of CDFW shares the story of the returning salmon, their life journey, and their connection to Sacramento's rivers at a family-friendly talk on the American River beach.

title: Tree Planting at Marshall Park
description: Neighbors will plant about 10 trees to replace ones lost in recent years. Bring a lunch and stay after to socialize and discuss ways to make Sacramento greener.

title: Pluckebaum Meet & Greet
description: Meet & greet with newly elected District 4 Councilmember Phil Pluckebaum, hosted by the Boulevard Park and Marshall-New Era neighborhood associations. Light refreshments provided.

title: Sage LaPena at Sutter's Landing
description: Guided plant walk with Nomtipom Wintu ethnobotanist and certified medical herbalist Sage LaPena, sharing her knowledge of the plants, place, and indigenous history of Sutter's Landing.

title: Spring Block Party & Seedling Swap
description: Family-friendly block party with music, food, and games celebrating the reopening of the New Era Community Garden, plus a seedling swap. Free admission; food truck 12–2:30 PM.

title: Community Yard Sale
description: Community-wide yard sale: sell from a stall at the flea market or from in front of your own house and be advertised on a yard sale map for a $10 fee.

title: CityConnect Monthly Mixer
location: The Lock & Key – Tapas + Bar, 2718 J St
description: City Community Engagement Team mixer to network with City staff, business owners, and fellow residents, celebrating the latest season of the SacVibes local government podcast. Food and admission free; RSVP requested.

title: National Night Out
location: 22nd St median, 2431 H St courtyard & Marshall Park
description: Progressive potluck dinner for National Night Out with appetizers at 6 PM, dinner at 7 PM, and dessert at 8 PM at three neighborhood locations. Bring a dish to share; city officials and police officers will attend.

title: Traffic Circle Clean-up
location: Meet at Marshall Park, 27th & I Sts
description: Volunteer cleanup of the traffic circles at 25th & G and 27th & F Streets, ahead of planting drought-tolerant native species this fall. Bring work gloves and water; RSVP to Mollie Quasebarth.

title: Water Wise Garden Showcase
location: Department of Utilities main campus, 1395 35th Ave
description: Free City event on saving water and money, with one-on-one access to gardening and irrigation experts, educational talks, rebate info, drought-tolerant garden tours, a native plant sale, and prizes.

title: Halloween Block Party
description: Family-friendly Halloween block party with music, food, and games, hosted by the Marshall-New Era and Boulevard Park neighborhood associations. Free admission; all residents welcome.

title: Councilmember Pluckebaum Town Hall
description: Town hall with District 4 Councilmember Phil Pluckebaum to learn what he is working on and discuss issues facing the district. Register via QR code.

title: Marathon Street Closures
location: J St, Alhambra Blvd & L St near the neighborhood
description: The 42nd California International Marathon will close J Street (Carlson to Alhambra), Alhambra (J Street to Capitol Ave), and L Street (Alhambra to 15th) from about 8:00 AM to 2:30 PM; the Capitol area is closed roughly 3 AM to 4 PM.

title: Native Planting at Sutter's Landing
location: Sutter's Landing, skate park parking lot
description: Join neighbors and ReWild Sacramento to plant natives, lay mulch, pull invasive weeds, and water previously planted wildflowers. Bring weather-appropriate clothing, water, gloves, and tools.

title: Spring Block Party
description: 2nd Annual Spring Block Party with music, food truck, games, and a plant/seedling swap, hosted by the Marshall-New Era and Boulevard Park neighborhood associations. Free admission; all residents welcome.

title: Environmental History of the Lower American River
description: A fun, educational morning at Sutter's Landing learning the history of the river, with an opportunity to help improve the landscape of the area.
